name: smart-ralph
description: Spec-driven development Claude Code plugin implementing the Ralph agentic loop pattern. Converts feature ideas into structured specs (research.md, requirements.md, design.md, tasks.md), then executes tasks one-by-one with fresh context per task. Includes PreToolUse, Stop, and SessionStart hooks for loop control.

findings_summary.notes: Well-structured spec-driven development plugin (MIT license, Jan 2026). Two plugin directories: ralph-speckit and ralph-specum. The hooks.json for ralph-specum defines three hooks: (1) PreToolUse on AskUserQuestion — runs quick-mode-guard.sh to enforce quick mode behavior, (2) Stop hook — runs stop-watcher.sh as a loop controller that checks for active spec and remaining tasks before allowing the agent to stop, (3) SessionStart — runs load-spec-context.sh to inject spec context. Reviewed stop-watcher.sh: it reads from local files, checks the .claude/ralph-specum.local.md settings file for enabled state, and uses jq to parse input — no network access, no credential handling. Has .agents/, .cursor/, .gemini/ directories for cross-tool compatibility. TROUBLESHOOTING.md present. CLAUDE.md at root. 9 open issues. No dangerous patterns identified. The Stop hook loop controller is legitimate and transparent — it blocks completion only when tasks remain in an active spec.
